expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Case focus is explicitly covered: blind scenario drafting captures prompt handling, template save, report builder loading, and visible report title outcomes
expectations.evidence: The result.md includes scenarios explicitly targeting all required focus areas: “Scenario 4a-1 — Prompt handling yields expected report title visibility”, “Scenario 4a-2 — Template save preserves report title and does not break subsequent loading”, and “Scenario 4a-3 — Report builder loading after prompt submit is reliable”. It also contains a “Coverage check against benchmark focus” section explicitly listing prompt handling, template save, report builder loading, and visible report title outcomes.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][advisory] Output aligns with primary phase phase4a
expectations.evidence: The document is titled “QA Plan Orchestrator — Phase 4a (Scenario Drafting)” and repeatedly frames the output as “Phase 4a Output: Blind Scenario Drafts”. It provides scenario drafts with Preconditions/Steps/Expected results, and uses evidence placeholders to comply with “blind_pre_defect”/“all_customer_issues_only” rather than inventing defect-specific details, which is consistent with phase4a scenario drafting constraints.
